title: calamagrostis phragmitoides var. elata
intro: Calamagrostis phragmitoides var. elata, also known as the tall fescue grass, is a type of perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of China. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial effects, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Calamagrostis phragmitoides var. elata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and soothe skin irritations, making it a popular ingredient in traditional Chinese medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions.
